title: Raw Refreshingly Fermented Callaloo Bowl
description: A Peruvian-inspired, fermented, and dairy-free-friendly solo-dinner featuring crisp callaloo and a refreshing twist.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine lime juice, apple cider vinegar, coconut water, and salt. Blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, combine callaloo greens, carrots, and red onion.
Pour the blended mixture over the greens mixture and toss to coat.
Pack the mixture into a large mason jar, pressing down to remove any air pockets.
Secure the jar with a lid and let it sit at room temperature for 24-48 hours. Shake the jar every 8 hours or so.
After 24-48 hours, the mixture should be slightly effervescent and have a sour taste. If it's too sour, you can let it sit for another 24 hours.
Transfer the fermented mixture to the refrigerator to slow down the fermentation process.
Chill for at least 2 hours before serving.
Serve chilled, garnished with additional callaloo greens if desired.
